nodes.title: Product Development & Niche Identification
nodes.rationale: Before investing time and resources, you need to define what makes your bakery unique. This module helps you identify your signature products and target audience, a critical first step for any beginner aiming to start a small business within 6 months. Podcasts on food entrepreneurship can offer great inspiration here.
suggested_micro_topics: Menu design and pricing strategy (initial thoughts)

nodes.title: Introduction to Small Business Concepts
nodes.rationale: As a beginner, understanding the fundamental language and principles of business is essential. This foundational module provides the basic vocabulary and mindset needed to approach the subsequent, more detailed business planning stages. Look for beginner-friendly business podcasts to grasp these concepts.
suggested_micro_topics: Basic financial statements overview (income, balance sheet)
Lean startup principles for food businesses

nodes.title: Baking Fundamentals & Recipe Scaling
nodes.rationale: Even with great ideas, the core skill of baking needs to be solid and scalable. This module focuses on mastering essential techniques and adapting recipes for commercial production, which is vital for a small bakery business. Practical hands-on learning combined with baking podcasts can accelerate this.
suggested_micro_topics: Understanding ingredient functions (flour, sugar, fat, leavening)
Introduction to specialty diets (gluten-free, vegan) if applicable

nodes.title: Developing Your Bakery Business Plan
nodes.rationale: With a 6-month limit, a clear, concise business plan acts as your roadmap, guiding decisions and attracting potential support. This module helps structure your vision into an actionable document, covering key areas from operations to finance. Many business podcasts offer templates and guidance for this.
suggested_micro_topics: Components of a business plan (executive summary, company description)
Funding request and investor pitch (if applicable)

nodes.title: Legal & Regulatory Framework for Food Businesses
nodes.rationale: Operating a food business requires strict adherence to health and safety regulations. This module ensures you understand the legal landscape to avoid costly mistakes and build a trustworthy business from day one. Researching local regulations is paramount.
suggested_micro_topics: Business registration and legal structure (sole proprietorship, LLC)
Local health department permits and inspections
Zoning laws for home-based vs. commercial kitchens

nodes.title: Financial Management & Pricing Strategies
nodes.rationale: To ensure profitability within your 6-month timeline, you must understand your costs and price your products effectively. This module covers essential financial skills for managing your bakery's money. Podcasts on small business finance can be very helpful.
suggested_micro_topics: Calculating startup costs and operating expenses
Cost of goods sold (COGS) for bakery items
Cash flow management for small businesses
Understanding profit margins and break-even analysis

nodes.title: Efficient Kitchen Operations & Advanced Food Safety
nodes.rationale: Once your recipes are solid, efficient and safe kitchen operations become key to consistent production and customer satisfaction. This module builds on basic baking and legal knowledge to create a professional kitchen environment. Food safety podcasts can keep you updated.
suggested_micro_topics: Essential bakery equipment selection and maintenance
Waste reduction strategies and sustainable practices

nodes.title: Marketing & Sales Strategies for Your Bakery
nodes.rationale: Even the best products won't sell themselves. This module focuses on attracting customers and building your brand, crucial for generating revenue quickly within your 6-month goal. Explore marketing podcasts for creative ideas.
suggested_micro_topics: Developing a bakery brand identity and story
Social media marketing for food businesses (Instagram, TikTok)
Local SEO and online presence (Google My Business)
Sales channels (farmers markets, online store, local cafes)

nodes.title: Sourcing, Supply Chain & Inventory Management
nodes.rationale: Managing ingredients efficiently impacts both product quality and profitability. This module helps you establish reliable supply chains and control inventory to minimize waste and optimize costs, directly supporting your 6-month business launch. Supply chain podcasts can offer insights.
suggested_micro_topics: Identifying reliable ingredient suppliers (local vs. wholesale)
Negotiating supplier contracts and bulk purchasing
Understanding lead times and delivery logistics

nodes.title: Launch Strategies & Early Growth Hacking
nodes.rationale: With your 6-month deadline approaching, this module focuses on the practical steps of launching your bakery and strategies for initial growth. It brings together all previous learning into actionable steps for opening your doors and attracting your first customers. Entrepreneurial podcasts often share launch stories and tips.
suggested_micro_topics: Soft launch vs. Grand opening planning
Collecting and acting on customer feedback
Hiring your first team members (if applicable)
suggested_micro_topics.title: Introduction to DevOps & CI/CD
suggested_micro_topics.rationale: Understanding the core principles of DevOps and CI/CD is fundamental before diving into specific tools like Kubernetes, as it provides the 'why' behind the practices.

suggested_micro_topics.title: Version Control with Git
suggested_micro_topics.rationale: Git is the industry standard for version control and is an absolute prerequisite for any CI/CD pipeline, as it manages the source code that pipelines operate on.
suggested_micro_topics: Git basic commands (add, commit, push, pull)

suggested_micro_topics.title: Containerization with Docker
suggested_micro_topics.rationale: Kubernetes orchestrates containers, so a solid understanding of Docker and containerization concepts is essential before learning Kubernetes itself.

suggested_micro_topics.title: Kubernetes Core Concepts
suggested_micro_topics.rationale: This module establishes the foundational knowledge of Kubernetes, which is critical for orchestrating applications and building CI/CD pipelines on it.

suggested_micro_topics.title: CI/CD Pipeline Tools & Concepts
suggested_micro_topics.rationale: Beyond the theoretical understanding of CI/CD, this module introduces the practical tools and concepts used to build and manage pipelines, which is directly relevant to securing a CI/CD job.

suggested_micro_topics.title: CI/CD Integration with Kubernetes
suggested_micro_topics.rationale: This module directly addresses the user's goal of getting a CI/CD job by focusing on how to integrate CI/CD pipelines with Kubernetes for automated deployments.
suggested_micro_topics: Deploying applications to Kubernetes via CI/CD
Image building and pushing to registry in pipeline
Secrets management in CI/CD for Kubernetes
GitOps principles (Argo CD, Flux CD introduction)

suggested_micro_topics.title: Helm for Kubernetes Package Management
suggested_micro_topics.rationale: Helm is a crucial tool for packaging and deploying applications on Kubernetes, making it an essential skill for managing complex CI/CD deployments.

suggested_micro_topics.title: Monitoring & Logging in Kubernetes
suggested_micro_topics.rationale: Effective monitoring and logging are critical for maintaining the health and performance of applications deployed via CI/CD on Kubernetes, and for troubleshooting pipeline issues.

suggested_micro_topics.title: Security in Kubernetes CI/CD
suggested_micro_topics.rationale: Security is paramount in any production environment. Understanding how to secure Kubernetes deployments and CI/CD pipelines is vital for a robust and reliable system.

suggested_micro_topics.title: Building a Complete Kubernetes CI/CD Pipeline (Project)
suggested_micro_topics.rationale: This capstone project integrates all learned concepts into a practical application, providing hands-on experience crucial for demonstrating skills for a CI/CD job.

{
  "nodes": [
    {
      "node_id": "devops_cicd_intro",
      "title": "Introduction to DevOps & CI/CD",
      "rationale": "Understanding the core principles of DevOps and CI/CD is fundamental before diving into specific tools like Kubernetes, as it provides the 'why' behind the practices.",
      "prerequisites": [],
      "suggested_micro_topics": [
        "What is DevOps?",
        "Benefits of DevOps",
        "CI/CD Pipeline overview",
        "Continuous Integration principles",
        "Continuous Delivery vs. Deployment",
        "DevOps culture and practices"
      ]
    },
    {
      "node_id": "git_fundamentals",
      "title": "Version Control with Git",
      "rationale": "Git is the industry standard for version control and is an absolute prerequisite for any CI/CD pipeline, as it manages the source code that pipelines operate on.",
      "prerequisites": [],
      "suggested_micro_topics": [
        "Git basic commands (add, commit, push, pull)",
        "Branching and Merging strategies",
        "Resolving merge conflicts",
        "Remote repositories (GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket)",
        "Git workflow (e.g., GitFlow, Trunk-based development)",
        "Reverting changes"
      ]
    },
    {
      "node_id": "docker_containerization",
      "title": "Containerization with Docker",
      "rationale": "Kubernetes orchestrates containers, so a solid understanding of Docker and containerization concepts is essential before learning Kubernetes itself.",
      "prerequisites": [],
      "suggested_micro_topics": [
        "What are containers?",
        "Docker architecture",
        "Dockerfile creation",
        "Building and managing Docker images",
        "Running Docker containers",
        "Docker Compose for multi-container apps",
        "Container registries (Docker Hub)"
      ]
    },
    {
      "node_id": "kubernetes_fundamentals",
      "title": "Kubernetes Core Concepts",
      "rationale": "This module establishes the foundational knowledge of Kubernetes, which is critical for orchestrating applications and building CI/CD pipelines on it.",
      "prerequisites": [
        "docker_containerization"
      ],
      "suggested_micro_topics": [
        "Kubernetes architecture (Master, Nodes)",
        "Pods, Deployments, Services",
        "Namespaces, Labels, Selectors",
        "kubectl commands",
        "YAML for Kubernetes manifests",
        "ReplicaSets and StatefulSets",
        "ConfigMaps and Secrets"
      ]
    },
    {
      "node_id": "ci_cd_tools_concepts",
      "title": "CI/CD Pipeline Tools & Concepts",
      "rationale": "Beyond the theoretical understanding of CI/CD, this module introduces the practical tools and concepts used to build and manage pipelines, which is directly relevant to securing a CI/CD job.",
      "prerequisites": [
        "devops_cicd_intro",
        "git_fundamentals"
      ],
      "suggested_micro_topics": [
        "Jenkins/GitLab CI/CD/GitHub Actions overview",
        "Pipeline as Code",
        "Build automation",
        "Testing in CI/CD (unit, integration)",
        "Artifact management",
        "Deployment strategies (blue/green, canary)",
        "Rollbacks"
      ]
    },
    {
      "node_id": "kubernetes_ci_cd_integration",
      "title": "CI/CD Integration with Kubernetes",
      "rationale": "This module directly addresses the user's goal of getting a CI/CD job by focusing on how to integrate CI/CD pipelines with Kubernetes for automated deployments.",
      "prerequisites": [
        "kubernetes_fundamentals",
        "ci_cd_tools_concepts"
      ],
      "suggested_micro_topics": [
        "Deploying applications to Kubernetes via CI/CD",
        "CI/CD pipeline stages for Kubernetes",
        "Using Kubeconfig in CI/CD",
        "Image building and pushing to registry in pipeline",
        "Automated testing in Kubernetes deployments",
        "Secrets management in CI/CD for Kubernetes",
        "GitOps principles (Argo CD, Flux CD introduction)"
      ]
    },
    {
      "node_id": "helm_package_management",
      "title": "Helm for Kubernetes Package Management",
      "rationale": "Helm is a crucial tool for packaging and deploying applications on Kubernetes, making it an essential skill for managing complex CI/CD deployments.",
      "prerequisites": [
        "kubernetes_fundamentals"
      ],
      "suggested_micro_topics": [
        "What is Helm?",
        "Helm Charts structure",
        "Creating and customizing Helm Charts",
        "Deploying applications with Helm",
        "Managing Helm releases",
        "Helm templates and values",
        "Helm repositories"
      ]
    },
    {
      "node_id": "kubernetes_monitoring_logging",
      "title": "Monitoring & Logging in Kubernetes",
      "rationale": "Effective monitoring and logging are critical for maintaining the health and performance of applications deployed via CI/CD on Kubernetes, and for troubleshooting pipeline issues.",
      "prerequisites": [
        "kubernetes_fundamentals",
        "kubernetes_ci_cd_integration"
      ],
      "suggested_micro_topics": [
        "Prometheus and Grafana for monitoring",
        "Kubernetes native logging (stdout/stderr)",
        "Elastic Stack (Elasticsearch, Fluentd, Kibana)",
        "Container insights",
        "Alerting strategies",
        "Tracing in Kubernetes"
      ]
    },
    {
      "node_id": "kubernetes_security_cicd",
      "title": "Security in Kubernetes CI/CD",
      "rationale": "Security is paramount in any production environment. Understanding how to secure Kubernetes deployments and CI/CD pipelines is vital for a robust and reliable system.",
      "prerequisites": [
        "kubernetes_fundamentals",
        "kubernetes_ci_cd_integration"
      ],
      "suggested_micro_topics": [
        "Image security scanning",
        "Secrets management best practices",
        "RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) in Kubernetes",
        "Network Policies",
        "Pod Security Standards",
        "Supply chain security in CI/CD",
        "Vulnerability scanning in pipelines"
      ]
    },
    {
      "node_id": "ci_cd_project",
      "title": "Building a Complete Kubernetes CI/CD Pipeline (Project)",
      "rationale": "This capstone project integrates all learned concepts into a practical application, providing hands-on experience crucial for demonstrating skills for a CI/CD job.",
      "prerequisites": [
        "kubernetes_ci_cd_integration",
        "helm_package_management",
        "kubernetes_monitoring_logging",
        "kubernetes_security_cicd"
      ],
      "suggested_micro_topics": [
        "Designing a multi-stage CI/CD pipeline",
        "Automating application deployment with Helm",
        "Implementing automated testing",
        "Integrating monitoring and logging",
        "Applying security best practices",
        "Troubleshooting pipeline failures",
        "Optimizing pipeline performance"
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

        "kubernetes_ci_cd_integration"
      ],

nodes.title: Product Development & Niche Identification
nodes.rationale: Before investing time and resources, you need to define what makes your bakery unique. This module helps you identify your signature products and target audience, a critical first step for any beginner aiming to start a small business within 6 months. Podcasts on food entrepreneurship can offer great inspiration here.
suggested_micro_topics: Menu design and pricing strategy (initial thoughts)

nodes.title: Introduction to Small Business Concepts
nodes.rationale: As a beginner, understanding the fundamental language and principles of business is essential. This foundational module provides the basic vocabulary and mindset needed to approach the subsequent, more detailed business planning stages. Look for beginner-friendly business podcasts to grasp these concepts.
suggested_micro_topics: Basic financial statements overview (income, balance sheet)
Lean startup principles for food businesses

nodes.title: Baking Fundamentals & Recipe Scaling
nodes.rationale: Even with great ideas, the core skill of baking needs to be solid and scalable. This module focuses on mastering essential techniques and adapting recipes for commercial production, which is vital for a small bakery business. Practical hands-on learning combined with baking podcasts can accelerate this.
suggested_micro_topics: Understanding ingredient functions (flour, sugar, fat, leavening)
Introduction to specialty diets (gluten-free, vegan) if applicable

nodes.title: Developing Your Bakery Business Plan
nodes.rationale: With a 6-month limit, a clear, concise business plan acts as your roadmap, guiding decisions and attracting potential support. This module helps structure your vision into an actionable document, covering key areas from operations to finance. Many business podcasts offer templates and guidance for this.
suggested_micro_topics: Components of a business plan (executive summary, company description)
Funding request and investor pitch (if applicable)

nodes.title: Legal & Regulatory Framework for Food Businesses
nodes.rationale: Operating a food business requires strict adherence to health and safety regulations. This module ensures you understand the legal landscape to avoid costly mistakes and build a trustworthy business from day one. Researching local regulations is paramount.
suggested_micro_topics: Business registration and legal structure (sole proprietorship, LLC)
Local health department permits and inspections
Zoning laws for home-based vs. commercial kitchens

nodes.title: Financial Management & Pricing Strategies
nodes.rationale: To ensure profitability within your 6-month timeline, you must understand your costs and price your products effectively. This module covers essential financial skills for managing your bakery's money. Podcasts on small business finance can be very helpful.
suggested_micro_topics: Calculating startup costs and operating expenses
Cost of goods sold (COGS) for bakery items
Cash flow management for small businesses
Understanding profit margins and break-even analysis

nodes.title: Efficient Kitchen Operations & Advanced Food Safety
nodes.rationale: Once your recipes are solid, efficient and safe kitchen operations become key to consistent production and customer satisfaction. This module builds on basic baking and legal knowledge to create a professional kitchen environment. Food safety podcasts can keep you updated.
suggested_micro_topics: Essential bakery equipment selection and maintenance
Waste reduction strategies and sustainable practices

nodes.title: Marketing & Sales Strategies for Your Bakery
nodes.rationale: Even the best products won't sell themselves. This module focuses on attracting customers and building your brand, crucial for generating revenue quickly within your 6-month goal. Explore marketing podcasts for creative ideas.
suggested_micro_topics: Developing a bakery brand identity and story
Social media marketing for food businesses (Instagram, TikTok)
Local SEO and online presence (Google My Business)
Sales channels (farmers markets, online store, local cafes)

nodes.title: Sourcing, Supply Chain & Inventory Management
nodes.rationale: Managing ingredients efficiently impacts both product quality and profitability. This module helps you establish reliable supply chains and control inventory to minimize waste and optimize costs, directly supporting your 6-month business launch. Supply chain podcasts can offer insights.
suggested_micro_topics: Identifying reliable ingredient suppliers (local vs. wholesale)
Negotiating supplier contracts and bulk purchasing
Understanding lead times and delivery logistics

nodes.title: Launch Strategies & Early Growth Hacking
nodes.rationale: With your 6-month deadline approaching, this module focuses on the practical steps of launching your bakery and strategies for initial growth. It brings together all previous learning into actionable steps for opening your doors and attracting your first customers. Entrepreneurial podcasts often share launch stories and tips.
suggested_micro_topics: Soft launch vs. Grand opening planning
Collecting and acting on customer feedback
Hiring your first team members (if applicable)
suggested_micro_topics.title: Introduction to DevOps & CI/CD
suggested_micro_topics.rationale: Understanding the core principles of DevOps and CI/CD is fundamental before diving into specific tools like Kubernetes, as it provides the 'why' behind the practices.
